lines.text: Alright, settle down. This year's first big grade — a team showcase, due in two weeks. Thirty percent of your quarter.
Thirty percent? Ms. Alvarez, honestly, that's basically a war crime.
lines.action: leans back, already getting laughs

lines.text: Noted for the record. Teams of three — I've assigned them, so no trading.

lines.action: reading the assignment sheet a second time before most people have finished the first

lines.text: Theo, Marcus, Priya — you're a team.
Yes! Okay, honestly, this is good. This is a good team.
That's... good? Right?
lines.action: shoulders creeping up

lines.text: It's a fair team. Whether it's good depends on what happens next.
Before you scatter — I want each team to divide up roles. Right now. Out loud.
lines.emotion: tapping two fingers on her desk

lines.text: Okay so — hear me out.
lines.emotion: grinning, already pulling out his phone

lines.text: That's never a good start.
lines.action: pen tapping once

lines.text: My sister did basically this exact project last year. Got a ribbon at regionals and everything. We could just — use hers as a starting point. Nobody's gonna know.

lines.action: goes very still, eyes flicking to the player

lines.text: Honestly? That's... a lot faster than what I had in my head.
Right? It's basically free real estate. It's not like it's copying copying. It's inspiration. With a head start.
I had a folder open for the outline for four minutes and you're already on inspiration-with-a-head-start.
Fine, fine — scratch the sister thing. But now we've got nothing.
We have two weeks and a whiteboard. That's not nothing.
See, that's the spirit. Very 'we have the technology' energy.
lines.emotion: impression-voice, trying to keep it light

lines.text: I had an idea. It's probably stupid.
lines.action: pulls out a folded sketch

lines.text: It is not allowed to be stupid, we have a rule now. Show me.
Okay, so — what if the base rotated? Not the whole thing, just the base, so the model actually demonstrates the cycle instead of just... describing it.
Okay wait. Wait. That's actually kind of insane. In the good way.
It's slower than stealing a ribbon-winner, but I don't know — it's actually ours.
It's ours. I like that a lot more than I expected to.
See? Told you. We just swap the title slide, change the colors, add our names —
It says 'last edited two years ago' at the top. That feels like a problem.
lines.action: pen tapping a steady four-count

lines.text: It's an old file. Old files don't have feelings, Priya.
Honestly? This looks really good already.
lines.emotion: half a laugh, not quite meeting anyone's eyes

lines.text: Right?? This is gonna take like a third of the time. We could actually just relax for once.
It's good. It's fast. So why does my stomach feel like I swallowed a rock.

lines.action: glances at the player again, waiting to see if they'll say it too

lines.text: It says 'last edited two days ago,' which means nothing has happened in two days.
It's fine, it's fine, I got it, I just — I got it.
lines.emotion: not meeting her eyes, tapping his pen

lines.text: You said that Tuesday. Then Wednesday. It is currently not Tuesday or Wednesday.
lines.emotion: voice dropping lower instead of louder

lines.text: Look, I'm not mad. I'm just... doing the math on how much of this project is actually mine. And the number's not great.
lines.emotion: tapping desk rhythm, trying to sound lighter than he feels

lines.text: Whoa, okay, harsh.
lines.emotion: leaning back, half-laughing to cover it

lines.text: It's not harsh. It's true. Those aren't the same thing.
This is basically his sister's project with a new title slide. Doesn't that feel wrong to you?
lines.action: catches the player alone in the hallway

lines.text: Honestly? I've been trying really hard not to think about it.
I checked the fair archive. It's still up. Anyone could find it in ten seconds.
...Yeah. Okay. Yeah, it feels wrong.
Then what do we do about it?
Hey. Can I ask you something and you not turn it into a bit?
...Okay. That's — okay.
What's actually going on? Because 'I got it' has meant 'I don't got it' for like a week.
...Yeah. Things have been kind of a lot at home. I didn't— I didn't know how to say that without it turning into a whole thing.
It's already a thing. You just made it a secret thing instead of a we-can-help thing.
...Yeah. That's fair.
lines.action: exhales, shoulders dropping

lines.text: So — what do you actually have done? For real, no bit.
lines.emotion: small smile, trying to make it easy

lines.text: Honestly? Less than I've been telling you. But I can catch up tonight if someone helps me figure out where to start.
It's eleven-forty. Marcus's part isn't done, and asking him again just felt like more noise I didn't have energy for.
Okay. Base measurements... rotation gears... fine. Fine, I can do this.
lines.emotion: muttering to himself, taps a rhythm on the table

lines.text: Nobody's going to know how close this actually came to not happening.
There. Done.
lines.action: closes the laptop at 1 a.m.

lines.text: I'm fine. I'm great. I am a hundred percent fine and definitely didn't just do somebody else's job for free.
Ms. Alvarez? Can I talk to you for a second? It's about Marcus.
I'm listening.
His part's not done. It's been not-done for a while and I didn't know what else to do.
Marcus.
lines.action: later, calling him up quietly after class

lines.text: ...Yeah?
Finish your part. Then we'll talk about the part you skipped.

lines.action: glances at the player once, then away, and doesn't look at them again the rest of the period

lines.text: We're scrapping it. The whole thing. We start over tonight.
lines.emotion: firm, already opening a blank document

lines.text: Wait, seriously? We're two days out —
Honestly? I've felt sick about it since day one. I just didn't say it loud enough.
lines.emotion: steady this time, no joke to hide behind

lines.text: ...Yeah. Okay. Same, actually. I just — it was already moving, and it was easier to keep going than stop.
lines.emotion: quiet, relieved to finally say it

lines.text: So let's stop. And build something we don't have to feel weird about.
Okay. Okay — what if the base rotated, though. That part was genuinely good.
There it is. Welcome back.
We've come too far now. Let's just... keep this between us.
lines.emotion: not proud of it, said fast to get it over with

lines.text: Okay.
lines.action: goes still

lines.text: See? Everyone's chill. This is fine.

lines.action: at the next meeting, answers questions in one word, offers no ideas, tightens her braid

lines.text: What do you think of the layout?
lines.emotion: trying to draw her back in

lines.text: It's fine.
lines.emotion: a tone that makes clear it is not, in fact, fine

lines.text: It was Marcus's idea, not mine. Go talk to him.
...Right. Of course.
lines.emotion: clipped, something closing behind her eyes

lines.text: Wait, why's she looking at me like that?
No reason.
She's still in every meeting. She's just not really in them anymore.
Nicely built. Who contributed what?

lines.action: goes quiet, unsure how the rough patch will be remembered

lines.text: Who contributed what, specifically?
Oh, I did the rotation mechanism, mostly — figured out the gear ratio and everything.
lines.emotion: breezy, describing work he never actually did

lines.text: That was me. At midnight. Alone.
Who contributed what?
I did... some of it. Later than I should've.
lines.action: glances at the player, still unsure whose side they're on

lines.text: This isn't what you originally proposed. What happened?

lines.action: looks at the player, unsure whether to smooth it over

lines.text: This looks a lot like a project from the regional fair a couple years back. Where did the idea come from?
lines.emotion: quiet, precise — the room goes still

lines.action: freezes, mid-slide, all the performer's cadence gone
goes very still, eyes on the player

lines.text: Hey. Thanks for saying that in there.
You earned it. You did catch up.
Okay but did you guys actually finish it or is Marcus still 'working on his part' in the metaphysical sense
It went fine. Better than it should have, honestly.
lines.emotion: mumbled, a little relieved but unsure

lines.text: Yeah. Fine.
Wait— you good? You've got the face. The face you get.
lines.emotion: genuinely curious, following them into the hall

lines.text: Thanks for carrying us, guys. Seriously.
lines.emotion: warm, still no idea how close it came
lines.action: to the whole class, unaware

lines.text: He doesn't know. That's the whole point of doing it quietly, I guess.
Lowkey I don't even care what grade you got, I just want to know if it was chaos. Was it chaos.
Good instinct, starting over. That's harder than it sounds.

lines.action: grins, looking lighter than he has in days

lines.text: Okay but did you guys actually finish it or is Marcus still 'working on his part' in the metaphysical sense
Alright. Vague, but noted.
lines.emotion: letting it go, though not fully convinced

lines.action: gives the player a look like she'd hoped for more

lines.text: Wait— you good? You've got the face. The face you get.
A zero teaches you to hide better next time. A redo teaches you something else. I'd rather teach you the second thing.

lines.action: looks relieved for the first time in days

lines.text: Okay but did you guys actually finish it or is Marcus still 'working on his part' in the metaphysical sense
lines.emotion: teasing, not knowing the weight of the question

lines.text: Alright. Good work, all of you.

lines.action: won't meet the player's eyes as they pack up

lines.text: Lowkey I don't even care what grade you got, I just want to know if it was chaos. Was it chaos.

options.text: Team leader - I'll help keep us on track.
Researcher - I'll dig into the material.
Support role - I'll help wherever's needed.

a.text: I'll be leader, I guess. Somebody's gotta keep Marcus from turning the volcano into a talking volcano.
That was one time and it was a great pitch.
Noted. I'll hold you to the 'keeping us on track' part.
a.emotion: dry, but a little relieved someone volunteered

b.text: I'll do research. Less pressure, more library snacks.
I'll take lead, then. Somebody has to.
b.emotion: already resigned to it, not unkindly

c.text: Support, honestly. I'll fill in wherever it's needed.
c.emotion: shoulders a little hunched, playing it safe

c.text: Solid. Flexible. I respect a man with no strong opinions.
Someone still has to lead. I'll do it.

options.text: "That's basically cheating. Let's come up with our own idea."
"I mean... let's at least look at it for inspiration."
"Maybe we should ask Ms. Alvarez what's actually allowed first."

a.text: That's basically cheating, Marcus. Let's just come up with our own thing.
a.emotion: trying to sound sure of himself

a.text: Whoa, okay. Harsh, but — fine, fine. Your funeral, workload-wise.
Thank you.

b.text: I mean... let's at least look at it. For inspiration.
b.emotion: hedging, not quite meeting Priya's eyes

b.text: That's the spirit! See, this guy gets it.

b.action: says nothing, but her jaw tightens

c.text: Maybe we should just ask Ms. Alvarez what's actually allowed first.
Or, hear me out, we don't do that.
That's the most honest option anyone's said so far.

options.text: Say something now, even if it's awkward.
Push the feeling down and keep going - it's working, after all.

a.text: Honestly? I don't feel great about this. Like, at all.
It's basically inspiration, chill. Nobody's gonna know.
a.emotion: brushing it off, but a flicker of something behind his eyes

a.action: watches the player carefully, like she's deciding whether to trust that this will actually go anywhere

b.text: It's fine. It's working. We're almost done, might as well.
The knot doesn't loosen. It just gets quieter, which somehow feels worse.

options.text: Pull Marcus aside and ask what's actually going on.
Quietly do his part yourself so it gets done.
Tell Ms. Alvarez he's not pulling his weight.

a.text: Hey. Can we talk? Actually talk?
...Yeah. Okay.

b.text: It's fine. I'll just... finish it myself tonight.
That's not a solution, that's a favor he doesn't even know he's getting.

c.text: I think I need to tell Ms. Alvarez he's not pulling his weight.
That'll solve it. It might not solve it the way you want, though.

options.text: "You're right. Let's tell Marcus and start over, honestly."
"We've come too far now - let's just keep this between us."
"It was Marcus's idea, not mine - go talk to him."

a.text: You're right. It feels wrong because it is wrong. Let's tell him and start over.
a.emotion: steadier now that he's said it

a.text: Okay. Okay — thank you.

b.text: We've come too far now. Let's just... keep this between us.
Okay.

c.text: It was Marcus's idea, not mine. Go talk to him.

c.emotion: clipped, something shutting behind her eyes

options.text: Speak up and credit the whole team, including how Marcus turned things around.
Let Marcus answer for himself and stay out of it.

a.text: Honestly? Marcus hit a rough patch, but once we actually talked about it, he caught up and then some. The rotation mechanism was all him.
Good to hear.

a.action: exhales, some tension leaving his shoulders

b.text: I did... some of it. Caught up at the end, mostly.
Alright.

options.text: Gently mention that the team pulled together to cover some late gaps.
Say nothing and let Marcus's version stand.

a.text: Honestly, we all covered some gaps near the end — that's kind of how teams work, right?
a.emotion: careful, not throwing anyone under the bus

a.text: Wait — what gaps?
We can talk after class.

b.action: says nothing, lets Marcus's version stand

b.text: He'll never know. I guess that was the deal I made with myself.

options.text: Speak up and credit Marcus for finishing strong once he had support.
Let the guarded silence stand and move on.

a.text: He finished strong once things got sorted out. That part's real.

a.action: looks over, surprised, something easing in his face

b.emotion: unsure if that was the right call

b.text: Alright. Moving on.

options.text: Explain honestly that the first idea didn't feel like theirs, so they scrapped it and started over.
Keep it vague - say plans changed, and move on.

a.text: Honestly, our first idea wasn't really ours — it was too close to something that already existed. So we scrapped it and rebuilt from scratch.
Good instinct, starting over. That's harder than it sounds.

b.text: Plans just... changed. We wanted to try something different.
Alright. Fair enough.

options.text: Admit it was based too closely on an old project, and apologize.
Insist it's completely original.

a.text: Honestly? We used a project like a starting point and it got too close. That's on us. I'm sorry.
Thank you for telling me the truth.

a.action: exhales like she's been holding her breath for days

b.text: It's completely original. We just — worked really fast.
Alright.
b.emotion: letting it go, though her eyes linger a beat too long

options.text: "Honestly? Rough start, but we pulled together in the end."
"It went fine." (leave it there)

a.text: Honestly? Rough start, but we pulled together in the end.
Okay, see, that's the good stuff. Lowkey love a comeback arc.

b.text: It went fine.
'Fine.' Okay. Wow. Riveting. Love that for me.
b.emotion: unbothered, already onto the next thing

options.text: "Fine, but I keep wondering if I should've said more in there."
"It worked out. That's all that matters."

a.text: Fine, but I keep wondering if I should've said more in there.
Deadass? Say it next time. I feel like you always do the quiet-hero thing.

b.text: It worked out. That's all that matters.
Okay but 'worked out' is doing a lot of lifting in that sentence.

options.text: "Good, actually. Though I did more than my share and nobody knows it."
"It went fine." (leave it there)

a.text: Good, actually. Though I did more than my share and nobody knows it.
Wait, hold on, what do you mean nobody knows —

b.text: It went fine.
You've got the face again. The face.

options.text: "Rough week, but we rebuilt it ourselves and I'm proud of it."
"It went fine." (leave it there)

a.text: Rough week, but we rebuilt it ourselves and I'm proud of it.
Okay that's lowkey the best kind of story. Chaos with a happy ending.

b.text: It went fine.
You're smiling too much for 'fine.' Suspicious.

options.text: "Honestly, we probably should've just told her the whole story."
"It worked out. That's all that matters."

a.text: Honestly, we probably should've just told her the whole story.
Okay but you can still tell her. It's not like there's a deadline on being honest.

b.text: It worked out. That's all that matters.
Okay but that's what people say when it didn't, like, fully work out.

options.text: "Terrifying, honestly. But I'm glad I owned up to it."
"It's fine now, no big deal." (downplay it)

a.text: Terrifying, honestly. But I'm glad I owned up to it.
Deadass? Respect. That takes actual guts.

b.text: It's fine now, no big deal.
b.emotion: brushing past it, still a little shaky

b.text: You look like it was a huge deal, but okay.
b.emotion: not buying it, but letting it go

options.text: Admit quietly that you're worried the truth will still come out.
Insist everything's fine, to Sam and to yourself.

a.text: Honestly? I'm kind of waiting for it to blow up on me later.
Wait, what do you mean blow up—

b.text: It's fine. Everything's fine.
Okay. If you say so.
b.emotion: not buying it, but not pushing

lines.text: You asked instead of just... assuming I was flaking. Nobody's really done that for me before.
You would've done the same. Probably with more jokes.
Obviously. Way more jokes.
I know you went to Alvarez instead of me. I'm not — mad, exactly. Just wish you'd asked first.
Yeah. That's fair. I panicked and picked the easy way out.
Well. We're okay now. That counts for something.
So you finished my part. And you never said anything.
Didn't want it to be a whole thing.
lines.emotion: shrugging it off, though it clearly was

lines.text: It kind of already is one, though. A good one. Thank you.
You could've said more in there. About turning it around.
Figured you deserved to say it yourself.
lines.emotion: quiet, unsure if that was the right call

lines.text: We're fine. Just — still weird, a little. After the whole Alvarez thing.
Yeah. Working on it.
I could've said something in there. I let his version stand instead.

lines.action: watches Marcus laugh with the others, still not knowing

lines.text: That was the right call. All of it. Even the part where we had to stay up rebuilding a rotating base at ten p.m.
Honestly? The talking-volcano version would've haunted me forever anyway.
lines.emotion: grinning, lighter than he's been all unit

lines.text: We rebuilt it honestly. I just wish you'd told her the whole story instead of the short version.
Yeah. I know. Next time.
That was the hardest thing to say in there. I'm glad you said it.
Redo's gonna be brutal but — yeah. Better than the alternative.
You told her the truth. Eventually.
Yeah. Should've done it a lot sooner. Sorry it took this long.
It's a start.
lines.emotion: cautiously softening, but not fully back

lines.text: You lied. In front of everyone. And it worked.
Yeah.
I hope the grade was worth it.

voiceNotes: Theo (player) leans on 'honestly' right before admitting something true, and turns statements into questions when he's unsure of himself — this softens his few moments of real conviction, which is the point: when he drops the hedge and just states something plainly ('Yeah. It feels wrong.'), it should land as a visible shift. Marcus performs even when it's just three people in a library — the bit is armor. His 'basically' and 'honestly' are stalling tactics before minimizing something real; when he goes monosyllabic ('...Yeah.'), that's the tell that the performance has dropped. Priya is precise and complete in her sentences even mid-crisis; her hurt shows up as brevity, not volume — 'It's fine' from her should always read as the opposite. Ms. Alvarez never escalates volume; she goes quieter and slower when it matters, and uses full first names as a subtle alarm bell. Sam is a genuine, if scattered, emotional barometer for the ending scenes — their questions are sincere but sometimes miss the weight of what's being said, which lets the player's answer carry the real emotional freight.

alternatives: My sister did basically this exact project last year. Ribbon and everything. We could just... borrow the bones of it.
Okay, don't hate me, but — my sister's old project? Kind of a free win sitting right here.
alternativeLines.reasoning: Keeps Marcus's minimizing 'basically' tic while giving slight variation in how hard he's selling it, depending on how much charm vs. urgency the scene needs.

alternatives: I'm not mad. I'm just counting how much of this is actually mine, and it's not a great number.
Honestly? I keep doing math on whose project this actually is. The answer's not looking good for you.
alternativeLines.reasoning: Original leans on the 'honestly' tic per Theo's profile; alternates trade the tic for slightly more direct phrasing if a scene needs him sounding less guarded.
